entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Incoming payment Internal Error 229 is showing.
entry.content:
Hi Debabrata 

I have received the same error. 

In my case it was missing permission of the RW and RO Database Users on the SBO-Common Database.
Please check the mapping and the permissions of the users. 

Let me know if that worked. 

Best regards
Maximilian

entry.title: Product Reservation for Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi Experts 

Good afternoon. 

We have below business requirement for sales order 

We will create a sales order along with a customer down payment. Once the customer pays the down payment amount, the product will be reserved for the sales order.

The reservation will have the following scenarios:

  • If the customer pays half of the payment, the stock will be reserved for one month.
  • If the customer pays the full amount, the stock will be reserved for three months.

The customer has to take delivery within the reservation period. If the reservation period exceeds, the customer will be charged 3% of the invoice value as a storage fee.

I am aware that, in the standard system, we don’t have any reservation facility. However, I am seeking an alternative solution for the above business requirement.

Thank You 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: Understanding Job Profile Acknowledgment in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Very well explained👌👌

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account (SAP Universal ID) and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Good Day,

I'm interested in joining the SAP Digital Skills Initiative program. As a young woman and single mother working in the construction industry in South Africa, I am deeply committed to advancing my career and embracing new opportunities that align with my aspirations.

The construction industry has provided me with valuable experience and skills, but I recognize that the future of my career lies in integrating digital skills into my professional toolkit. The SAP Digital Skills Initiative represents a pivotal opportunity for me to bridge the gap between traditional industry practices and the evolving demands of the digital age.

entry.title: Dashboard for daily Basis monitoring
entry.content: I would like to create a dashboard for the daily monitoring activities such as dumps, system errors and failed jobs, I have tried creating the same in the OCC dashboard of focused insight. But I am not getting the values as expected. For example number of short dumps for today will give correct value, but if wanted to view the dumps for 2 days or yesterday the given value is wrong like rational value(I am using system monitoring as data provider and metric name as ABAP_SYS_SHORT_DUMPS_TODAY).

entry.title: Re: Link Sales Order & Purchase Order in SAP Standard Rep...
entry.content:
Hi 

Goto SE16 - VBFA table....and in the field 'subsequent document' - give the PO number and execute.

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Link Sales Order & Purchase Order in SAP Standard Rep...
entry.content: Hello, Can you please Suggest any SAP Standard Report to get the details of Linking of Purchase order and sales Order ?

entry.title: Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
@Tunir 

 

 

Thanks for your answer . I did some converting tests with the QR3 release of SAC and you're right. Ranking works with chart now.

I still have  some strange behavior when i converted some stories (for example some of styling rule or formats applied in cells changed , some stories needs to be saved again after converting in order to see everything in inputs control in view mode ) but it finally works .

entry.title: Date Format Need to Change in talent profile performance history portlet, only year.
entry.content:
Hi All , 

We have a requirement from business to change the date format in employee profile under  performance history portlet.

Business need a only year(YYYY) instead of ( DD/MM/YYYY), in performance history portlet.

We have checked all the possibility but we not able to modify and achieve this request. 

Please do let know is anyone have a same request for your respective business and how we can fulfill this requirement or do we have any workaround.

 

Best Regards, 

Dhanush Kumar

entry.title: Old requirement type in md61
entry.content:
I tried planning strategy 40 for a material and maintained PIR in md61. At beginning the requirement type was VSF. Later changed the strategy to 10 for same material in MM02 and tried to maintain PIR. But this time got 2 line items in MD61.

At first tried deleting the old requirement line item and created new line item. But still the PIR was created with old Requirement type.
Ran MD74, MD75, MD76. I have not deleted the old PIR line item.
Later change the user parameter in MD61 and changed the requirement type.

Why this is happening. Is it system behavior. Whenever we change the strategy the system should change the requirement type for MD61 based on strategy right?

entry.title: SAP IBP Optimizer doesn't give a solution while the infinite heuristic does
entry.content:
For some reason, the optimizer does not calculate any dependent demands and production receipts at all, while the infinite heuristic works just fine.

This is the case: 

The header material has independent demand, is subcontracted externally and its SFG's should be produced in-house in a different location. 

The optimizer doesn't create dependent demand going to these SFG's while the infinite heuristic does. 

* The capacity is available on these in-house Resources

* The RM's are not optimizer relevant so these aren't considered as a constraint to the optimizer

* The transportation lanes and costs are defined

The message I get is this: "can't perform sourcing for missing supply of X,XX in time, because of the planning horizon."

 

What's going on and who could help me? Please be specific since I'm still new to IBP.

entry.title: Re: PDF file uploading by DTW in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi,

You can refer this for the same

https://forum.sapb1.vn/community/dtw/cach-import-attachment-cho-doi-tuong-item-master-data-bang-dtw/

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Re: L_TO_CONFIRM - SU Managed Bulk with differences
entry.content: Did you solve this issue? If so, can you share the solution?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Can you check your customizing for movement types: SPRO>Quality Management>Quality Inspection>Inspection lot creation>Inspection for Goods Movements>Movement Type/Update Control/Inspection Lot Origin. For movement 453 the Inspection lot origin should be 05 in the standard system settings for all variants of the 453 movement.

If you have 05 here but still get a 08 lot triggered there is something happening in the system that is not standard.

entry.title: crystal report viewer not showing images
entry.content:
Hi,

We are using Crystal Report as a report in Visual Studio 2022.
We have purchased SAP Crystal Report 2020 for development additionally we installed
Crystal report Service Pack 36 runtime 32 and 64-bit.
 
The Crystal Report Viewer is not showing Images. In the XSD file, we have set System.Byte[] Datatype for the Signature column.
For More detail see attached screenshot

entry.title: Re: I can't create a MDK Project
entry.content: Have you verified you have your app configured in Mobile Services correctly and are logged into the correct org/space in BAS?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
eel free to include a bit about yourself like academic / professional background and why you feel you are a good fit to the program.

I'm a single mom that has a BA in Accounting and an MBA.  I spent the 1st 10 years of my career in the finance function supporting various manufacturing companies.  I decided I wanted to switch to IT because I learn new systems quickly and find the work very interesting.  I love problem solving and I find IT work is a lot like completing a puzzle, you start with tons of tiny pieces of information and once they are all put together, they become a connected picture. 

The companies I've worked for have used SAP and I can see many ways for my new company to use SAP better.  I'd like to be part of this program to become certified in the technical aspects of SAP.

entry.title: Error when trying to create a scheduled publication in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

When I try to create a schedule using the File -> Schedule publication option in story, I am getting the error shown below. This is not happening with all stories. Please suggest.

entry.title: Re: UDF Setup history item list BP ordered
entry.content:
Hi Marc,

try this

SELECT
	 [RDR1].[ItemCode]
	,[RDR1].[Dscription]
	,SUM([RDR1].[Quantity]) AS [Sum]
	,MAX([ORDR].[DocDate])
FROM
	[ORDR]
	INNER JOIN [RDR1] ON [RDR1].[DocEntry] = [ORDR].[DocEntry] 
WHERE
	[ORDR].[CardCode] = $[$4.0.0]
	AND
	[ORDR].[DocDate] Between DATEADD(dd,-180,GetDate()) AND GetDate() 
GROUP BY 
	[RDR1].[ItemCode],[RDR1].[Dscription]

I recommend

https://www.google.com/search?q=mastering+sql+queries+for+sap+business+one&sca_esv=5e78618b726d8b1c&rlz=1C1GCEU_deDE921DE921&ei=9gzXZvmcBYiL7NYPh-2A2Qs&oq=mastering+sql+queries&gs_lp=Egxnd3Mtd2l6LXNlcnAiFW1hc3RlcmluZyBzcWwgcXVlcmllcyoCCAEyBxAAGLADGB4yBxAAGLADGB4yBxAAGLADGB4yBxAAGLADGB4yBxAAGLADGB4yCxAAGLADGKIEGIkFMgsQABiABBiwAxiiBEjHG1AAWABwAXgAkAEAmAEAoAEAqgEAuAEByAEAmAIBoAIFmAMAiAYBkAYHkgcBMaAHAA&sclient=gws-wiz-serp 

so you can make so small adjustments on your own.

Keep in mind that the item-description in a document, can differ from ItemName

regards Lothar

Thanks for help, Thomas

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

 Thank you very much for letting us know details about the Digital Skills Initiative program. I identify as a women in a developing country and this program opens the opportunity for me to the SAP consultant world. Once again, thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Interact programmatically with SAP CAP framework's No...
entry.content:
Thanks for your suggestion Gregor! It looked promising and I tried it, but unfortunately it didn't work for my use-case. Mine differs to yours in the following ways:

  • I want to run the script interact-with-cap.js from a subfolder of my project, but I would like to use the settings from the CAP project in the root folder.
  • My settings include connections to bound cloud services, like a HANA DB. All the setups I tried so far, didn't resolve the bound services correctly.

I wanted to run my script in a subfolder with a given configuration profile:

cd migration
node interact-with-cap.js --profile my-profile

My project layout looks like this:

<project root>
├── app
├── db
├── migration
│   └── interact-with-cap.js
└── srv

Currently I'm stuck and not sure if it makes sense to proceed with my approach. It seems to me the CAP startup process is rather entangled and not easy to get right without deep knowledge about inner workings and reading the framework's sourcecode. Especially when reading comments like these in several places:

  • cds.env = cds.env.for('cds') // reload env // FIXME: This is too early!
  • const DEBUG = cds.debug('cli') // REVISIT: This loads cds.env too early!

My hope was that I could do something like const myenv = cds.init(__dirname + '../', { profile: 'my-profile' }) and interact with CAP like a configured library. But I looks like spinning up the whole server is the only official entry point to the framework.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Good Day @David_Chaviano ,

I'm interested in joining the SAP Digital Skills Initiative program. As a young woman and single mother working in the construction industry in South Africa, I am deeply committed to advancing my career and embracing new opportunities that align with my aspirations.

The construction industry has provided me with valuable experience and skills, but I recognize that the future of my career lies in integrating digital skills into my professional toolkit. The SAP Digital Skills Initiative represents a pivotal opportunity for me to bridge the gap between traditional industry practices and the evolving demands of the digital age.

Thank you

PearlSerapelo

entry.title: Re: The token in the OpenDocument URL does not work
entry.content:
I see two potential issues.  First, check to ensure that you have enabled token logon for openDocument, since it is disabled by default.  Assuming you're using Tomcat, look in webapps/BOE/WEB-INF/config/custom.  You should have an openDocument.properties file in there containing "logontoken.enabled=true".  If not, add it and restart Tomcat.  

Next, depending on how you retrieved the token from the REST call, you may not need to encode it.  If it still doesn't work after enabling logontoken, try your call without UrlEncode().

entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is it possible to create a table of contents in the DM Word document?
We are using word styles similar to standard Word table of content structures?

If so, any guidance would be appreciated.

Thanks

Eyal

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: Task 3 Submission

entry.title: Only able to successfully call api '/raylight/v1/about' all other calls receive 401
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm only been able to call '/raylight/v1/about' with success. all other calls e.g /session return 401

I have been able to authenticate and return a token which I use in the subsequent calls. Below is a pared down version of the code in C#:

 

using (var client = new System.Net.Http.HttpClient())
{
    client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Clear();
    client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization = new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", token);
    client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Accept.Add(new MediaTypeWithQualityHeaderValue("application/xml"));
    var response = await client.GetAsync("http://servername:8080/biprws/raylight/v1/session");
                
    GD.Print("response" + response);
    return "ok";
 }

 

I have administrator privileges. I was wondering if I needed to do some further setup in the system or the token format is not correct? The token is taken from this response:

 

 responseStatusCode: 200, ReasonPhrase: '', Version: 1.1, Content: System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionResponseContent, Headers:
{
  Date: Tue, 03 Sep 2024 06:13:08 GMT
  X-SAP-LogonToken: "everything between the double quotes is extracted"
  Vary: accept-encoding
  Transfer-Encoding: chunked
  Content-Type: application/xml
}

 

Any help would be appreciated.

update: I think I am missing something like this

client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Add("X-SAP-LogonToken", $"\"{token}\"");

or

client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Add("X-SAP-LogonToken", token});

instead or along with

client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization = new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", token);

 

Thanks,

Paul

entry.title: Re: Can you trust Pam?
entry.content: Shall anyone knows how i get access to PAM?

entry.title: Re: Connecting to MySQL from CAPM application
entry.content:
If you want to use the onPrem MySQL DB as your CAP persistence you need to check if https://github.com/Soontao/cds-mysql would support connecting to the DB via Cloud Connector. To support this a TCP Endpoint (the MySQL Server) must be exposed in SAP Cloud Connector and the cds-mysql adapter would need to support connections via the SOCKS5 proxy.

If that is not an option you could try to run a CAP application using the cds-mysql adapter onPrem and consume its exposed APIs via the Destination.

entry.title: What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
entry.content:
Fall is coming and the next TechEd Virtual is already in preparation. Take the chance and flip through the session catalog for all the interesting sessions of our integration track. Register now and join us online October 8-9, 2024. 

As usual find with this blogpost the highlights of our SAP Integration Suite increments that have been released in August:

  • Get ready with custom and purchased adapters for the Apache Camel 3.14 upgrade
  • Edit namespace for message types
  • Persistence in MariaDB databases
  • Principal propagation as authentication method to connect with on-premise systems
  • New parameters in SFTP and AMQP sender adapters
  • Inspection of content size
  • Diagnostic tasks in operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell
  • Community integration package for extension points
  • Appropriate time zone in B2B monitor
  • Suborganizations in company profiles
  • Support of EDI flat-file payloads
  • Integration Advisor migration guide
  • Envelope templates for EDI messages
  • New version of the Process Integration pipeline concept
  • Adapter modules
  • Manage content from API business hub enterprise
  • Default credential type for service key

A2A integration

Get ready with custom and purchased adapters for the Apache Camel 3.14 upgrade

As the Apache Camel upgrade is happening soon, do some prerequisites to run your integration scenarios before your productive tenants are updated to the new runtime. Please upload a Camel 3.x compatible version of your custom adapters and of adapters you have purchased from other vendors to your productive tenant. A special area is available for upload. By uploading your adapters, we ensure that they will get ported automatically during the update.

With regards to OEM adapters by partners, please run the latest Camel 2.x compatible versions. An upload isn’t necessary.

To avoid any business disruption with unexpected integration flow behaviour, we recommend an upfront validation of your custom adapters. For this purpose you may leverage the free 90 days valid test tenant that already offer for quite some time. It runs on Apache Camel 3.14 where you can test your adapters and integration flows.

Ensure that also your design time has the latest version of the adapters and upload the .esa files to the appropriate integration packages. For more details, see SAP Note #3326553.

Edit namespace for message types

The recently introduced message types have been extended in the way that you can now view the namespace to which a message type belongs to and edit and create a new one. Refer the documentation.

Persistence in MariaDB databases

To support our SAP Process Orchestration customers, we have extended the Java database connectivity (JDBC) receiver adapter by supporting now also MariaDB. The adapter is available in multi-cloud environments and in Edge Integration Cell runtime for ground-to-ground integration scenarios. See the documentation.

Principal propagation as authentication method to connect with on-premise systems

You can now propagate the principal of the inbound technical user with the principal propagation authentication method in the HTTP receiver adapter. The tenant authenticates itself against the receiver by forwarding the principal of the inbound user to the Cloud Connector, and from there to the back end of the relevant on-premise system. Check out the documentation.

New parameters in SFTP and AMQP sender adapters

We have introduced new parameters in the SFTP and AMQP adapters. The SFTP sender adapter is extended by Poll on one worker only used to specify that the polling process is performed on a single worker node only. The AMQP sender adapter that is consuming messages from queues in an external message broker offers now the parameter Disable Reply-To allowing you to disable the reply back for a message.

Inspection of content size

The powerful inspect feature to analyze your resource consumption has been extended. You can now inspect the file size of all the integration artifacts (such as integration flows, value mappings, adapters) they allocate on the tenant they are deployed on. You may detect the top consumers and in case of integration flow artifacts switch also between the various inspect categories to detect the consumption of other relevant database and system resources. Refer the Inspect Content Size.

 

Integration in private landscapes

Diagnostic tasks in operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell

Integration administrators can run diagnostic tasks in the operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell. You may increase the log level configuration temporary with only few clicks, and with the Takt 2408 in September you may either trigger and view thread and heap dump on Java components. The diagnostic data can be collected on a file system and shared with SAP Support for troubleshooting. Check out the documentation.

B2B integration

Community integration package for extension points

The B2B Integration Factory integration packages are available for customers that require additional extension points and custom features that are not yet provided by the standard TPM solution. If the standard TPM solution does not cover your integration requirements, use the community packages instead.

The following packages are available:

  • Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management
  • Communication Receiver Flows - Custom
  • Communication Sender Flows
  • Interchange Extraction Flows
  • Extended Interchange Processing Flows
  • Pre and Post-Processing Flows - Custom

Appropriate time zone in B2B monitor

To enhance efficiency in B2B monitoring, dates and times are now displayed according to your browser's time zone, rather than UTC. Additionally, you can easily rearrange field positions using drag and drop, and sort the columns in the interchange list. For more information, see monitor interchanges and monitor unassigned interchanges.

Suborganizations in company profiles

Within the Trading Partner Management subsidiaries are supported. You can now create and maintain suborganization profiles as children of the main company profile. They may have their own configuration requirements, such as certificates, systems, and communication channels. Check out the documentation for more details.

Support of EDI flat-file payloads

For payload XML was the only supported format by the Integration Advisor capability so far. To increase your efficiency, you can now use the text-based flat-file EDI format (used for X12, UN/EDIFACT, and Tradacoms) as well. EDI payloads can be uploaded as MIGs, and you can also use the payload during the MIG and MAG simulation. The conversion of the EDI payload into its XML representation is implicitly done by Integration Advisor. Check out all the details in the blogpost.

Integration Advisor migration guide

We have now published a migration guide for the Integration Advisor capability that details the steps needed for customer planning to migrate their design time artifacts from the Neo environment to SAP Integration Suite.

Envelope templates for EDI messages

The EDI standards X12 and UN/EDIFACT have so called envelopes that are wrapped around the actual messages. You can now define Message Implementation Guidelines (MIGs) that combine envelope and message structure and share this documentation with business partners. For the definition you can leverage envelope templates as defined by the standards. The envelope template content will be released gradually over the next few weeks, starting with UN/EDIFACT and ASC X12, followed by EDIFACT subsets and Odette. See the details in the documentation.

Migration from SAP Process Orchestration

New version of the Process Integration pipeline concept

To run your asynchronous message processing in Cloud Integration in a similar way as you do it in SAP Process Orchestration, we have introduced the pipeline concept. Now we have released a version 1.0.6 of the package with some enhancements. To offer a way to define sender wildcard scenarios we have revised the partner ID definition and support also alternative partners to overcome partner id restrictions. Another enhancement is that the receiver and interface determination can now be bypassed in pure point-to-point scenarios to improve the runtime performance. Read the very detailed blog post and see the pipeline package published at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. And if you like to make yourself familiar check out the Process Integration pipeline sample scenarios in the GitHub repository including also a guideline how to  the pipeline concept

Adapter modules

The message transformation utilities community package contains artifacts that help transforming message payloads to various formats, such as XML, plain text, CSV, and JSON. The package delivers various functionalities that once existed in SAP Process Orchestration as adapter modules. The utilities covered so far are MessageTransformBean and XMLAnonymizer.

API-managed integration

Manage content from API business hub enterprise

You can now publish selected SAP system APIs directly into the API business hub enterprise catalog, without first defining API proxies for these APIs. To accomplish this, your global account administrator first needs to register these systems in your tenant. The registered systems then appear in the manage content page of API business hub enterprise, from which you can then publish the APIs directly. See the documentation.

Default credential type for service key

In the API business hub enterprise, we've updated the default credential type for the service key. It's now binding-secret instead of instance-secret. We made this change because xsuaa has deprecated the credential type instance-secret. See more details in our documentation.

How to stay tuned to recent and upcoming innovations?

The SAP Road Map Explorer is your one-stop shop for all SAP Integration Suite innovations. You can easily check out the latest innovations and follow what is planned for the following quarters. All recent innovations also cover under the tab Features further links to blogposts or documentation.

We also refer to the complete list of new releases in our documentation: What’s New in SAP Integration Suite.

And if you have not heard of our monthly webinars, I suggest you sign up to get an invitation to the upcoming ones. Our team of Product Management experts host these webinars to showcase the latest and greatest updates regarding all SAP Integration Suite capabilities. The webinars are hosted on the last Tuesday of every month and the next one is already scheduled for September 24th.

In case you have missed our last monthly webinar, don’t worry. Visit 2024 Learning Sessions for SAP User Groups on SAP Integration Suite for all recordings, PDFs, and Q&As.

Are you aware of the Release Navigator for SAP BTP? It consolidates release information across SAP BTP products and services easing you the way to find product release related notes, blogposts, and webpages. For your convenience use the direct link to the SAP Integration Suite section of the Release Navigator.

entry.title: Re: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content:
Hello Hanumanth,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Our expert, Jakob, has addressed most of your queries. Are you still encountering any unresolved issues? Additionally, I recommend following our help portal for SAP Build Process Automation  - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation?locale=en-US  for any further questions.

entry.title: Re: Special Release Linux on IBM Power Little Endian for SAP NetWeaver 7.4 application servers
entry.content: You need a S-USER and access the portal SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One – Formatted Searches and How to Find Them
entry.content:
More than welcome Santos - glad to hear it helped out.  I also have first-hand knowledge of how frustrating it can be when doing a migration and trying to manually figure all of this out.  The main reason it was written.

Regards to you in Brazil - Zal  &:-D

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process Accelerator VS other SAP Info So...
entry.content:
Dear JD_WongLoera, 

thank you for your question and apologies for the delay in response. As this is such an extensive question related to different sources of information, it is difficult to answer it in a concise manner in this threat with all the information you desire. 

I would like to point you to our upcoming webcast How to leverage SAP Signavio Value Accelerators with the Value Accelerator Library on the 24th of September 2024. This would be the perfect opportunity to exchange with our experts also on the differences in the content as raised by you. 

You can also find a blog on the Value Accelerator Library in our Community and interact with our experts there. 

Please note, to access the webcast link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide to registration can be found here.

Best regards, 

Katie

entry.title: Free Of Charge Process Public Cloud Sales
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using the CBXN item type for the CBFD document type in the Free of charge process. In my pricing schema, there are D100 conditions with routines 55 and 28. Subsequently, I want to create a proforma for delivery using F8, but the amount is not showing as 0. Is there any alternative routine I could use?

entry.title: Re: Details of PR not showing on MY Fiori Inbox SAP S4/HANA
entry.content: Hi, Yes you need to maintain that entry for your workflow ID which is starting with WS.

entry.title: How to Import an UBL XSD Structure in SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

I have a request to transform an invoice into an XML file; however, this XML must comply with the UBL standard. I understand that with the transaction STRANS I can include a Transformation to fill a structure automatically, nevertheless, When I see the UBL XSD Schema, to create a structure manually on the Tcode SE11, seems to be a wrong approach. Does anyone know a way to include or upload the UBL XSD Schema for the automatic creation of the structure in the DDIC ? If someone can send me a step by step, or any Information, it will be very appreciated. 

 

In some threads I red that with Eclipse is also possible, unfortunately I'm not familiarized with Eclipse 😞 

 

Thanks a lot in advance,

Lee

entry.title: Re: Solman_setup 500 Internal Server Error
entry.content: Thanks for your reply but it was hostname parameter issue. But still thanks for your time. Hope you have great day

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload of Test Case into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Thanks Ajay, this is really helpful, pls let us now once mass upload is released..

entry.title: Remove 1st rehire verification check and only use the 2nd rehire verification?
entry.content:
Has anyone removed the 1st rehire verification check and only used the 2nd rehire verification check?  We only want to have a rehire verification done once the candidate has provided their personal data.  We don't want the system to perform the 1st rehire verification check as it holds up the candidate getting their welcome email and delays the onboarding process.

Thanks 

Kristi Thomas

entry.title: Re: How to Integrate two different Custom Api in eclipse?
entry.content: you mean you don't know RAP EML?

entry.title: Re: Using Signavio Process Governance Event Data in Proce...
entry.content:
Dear spartan_2000,

thank you for your question. 

The automatic transfer of data from SAP Signavio Process Governance into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence (or SAP Signavio Process Insights) is currently not possible. We would be more than happy to add this as a feature request for our product team to evaluate. 

To do so, could you please send an email to signavio_success@sap.com with your request and the benefits / your specific use case for this feature. As described in your post the benefit would be the possibility to analyze the data on a real-time basis rather than importing data manually ever so often. Any further information you can provide for this feature to be beneficial for you will help our product team to understand and evaluate the feature. 

Thank you so much in advance, 

Katie

entry.title: Re: External Budget Check Response Failed
entry.content: Try to diable CIG and Re-enable CIG,  this should refresh the endpoints in the backend.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration: Call iFlow | Basic Authentication | user from Azure Active Directory | IAS as
entry.content: @CarlosRoggan, at the beginning of this post you mention the case of iFlow endpoints being protected with OAuth.  In that scenario, would there be any security reasons to add an API overlay to the same OAuth-protected endpoint using SAP API Management?

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on parent entity field value
entry.content:
You can achieve this through 'default function' functionality. In  bdef of your parent entity, define default function to pass the parent field value to associated child. then This will by default filter the value help result based on parent entity field. Example:

Step1. Add association  _childEntity { create{ default function GetDefaultsForChildEntity;} with draft; }

Step 2. Define this method in Behv Impl Class

  METHODS GetDefaultsForChildEntity FOR READ

IMPORTING keys FOR FUNCTION ParentEntity~GetDefaultsForChildEntity RESULT result.

Step 3. implement this function in behv Impl class.      

READ ENTITIES OF r_ParentEntity

IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY ParentEntity(Alias of parent entity)

FIELDS ( Parent_entity_field_name )

WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )

RESULT DATA(lt_data).

CHECK lt_data[ 1 ] IS NOT INITIAL.

result = VALUE #( %param-Parent_entity_field_name = lt_data[ 1 ]-Parent_entity_field_name

( %tky = CORRESPONDING #( keys[ 1 ] ) ) ).

 

Step-3. Done. this will by default filter child entity VH result based on value of Parent_entity_field_name.

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content: Does that target cds have lineitem annotation?

entry.title: Re: I want to connect SAC to my on premise SAP HANA database
entry.content: It would help if you share what connection method you use (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/cc03773d7f554c4ebc9e29b4c2792e26.html?version=release&locale=en-US) and what error message you are getting.

entry.title: Re: How to release a retention payment during Payment Run
entry.content: Thank you for the help here. We are actually inputting a Retention Due Date during the IR but when we run normal payment processing after that, it is not picking up the invoice.

entry.title: Re: Duplicidade dados NF entrada - J1B1N
entry.content:
Solução standard:

Aplicar SAP notes: 1614648 e 1606367 (INCLUDE LJ1BB2FF0) 

Atualizar visão T160M com a mensagem 8B 669.

Ao gravar a NF, irá mostrar a mensagem.

Hi @SonicPlanet

entry.title: SAML2 service not accessibleWhat has happened?Calling of URL https://myxxxx.scmibp1.ondemand.c
entry.content:
SAML2 service not accessible

What has happened?

Calling of URL https://myxxxx.scmibp1.ondemand.c was terminated during SAML2 processing

 Note

  • No RelayState mapping found for RelayState value oucxzzvvwvaoewtboreefozabfoyazwwzyeawuw

 

HTTP 404 Not Found

#FSV

#FSE2

#OB58

SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Cálculo DIFAL Base Dupla
entry.content:
Pode implementar a regra utilizando a BADI:
DIFAL - BADI_J1B_DIFAL_RECALC - Método RECALCULATE

entry.title: Re: PO not sent to ariba network
entry.content:
Hello,

The error could appear if the trading relationship with your supplier was interrupted by them. Try resending the supplier a TRR (https://me.sap.com/notes/KB0395260) and then resend the order, which can be done via Enhanced Resending of Purchase Orders Stuck in Ordering or Cancelling State (https://me.sap.com/notes/KB0717382).

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content: I am excited to participate, this is my first year. 😁

entry.title: Re: External storage permission in android
entry.content:
Hello Marajah,

Thank you for reaching out to us.

To remove the permission, you can use the App_Resources_Merge functionality:

  1. Create the following file under your .mdkproject folder:
css
Copy code
<yourproject>.mdkproject/App_Resources_Merge/Android/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml
  1. Add the following content to the AndroidManifest.xml:
xml
Copy code
<manifest package="__PACKAGE__" android:versionCode="1" android:versionName="1.0" xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android" xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"> <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:node="remove"/> </manifest>
  1. Finally, rebuild your client using the create-client command.

Let me know if you need any further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Abap program to end specific User's Program, by sesion/tcode or GUIID

entry.title: Potenzial entfalten mit dem SAP Builder Day: Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP Build
entry.content:
Bereit, die Entwicklungsfähigkeiten auf die nächste Stufe zu heben? Egal, ob erfahren oder gerade erst am Anfang, der SAP Builder Day: Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP Build am 26. September und 12. November 2024 bietet die perfekte Gelegenheit, Fähigkeiten zu erweitern und Ideen zum Leben zu erwecken.

Warum am SAP Builder Day teilnehmen?

  1. Beschleunigte Lernkurve

Low-Code-Entwicklung ist die Zukunft. Dieser Kurs bietet einen schnellen, effizienten Weg, um die wesentlichen Konzepte von SAP Build zu erlernen und innerhalb kurzer Zeit Fähigkeiten zu erwerben, die normalerweise Wochen des traditionellen Lernens erfordern.

  1. Praxiserfahrung

Theorie ist gut, aber Praxis ist Gold wert. Der SAP Builder Day besteht nicht nur aus Demos, sondern auch aus praktischen Übungen, realen Anwendungsfällen und interaktiven Sitzungen, sodass anwendbare Kenntnisse gewonnen werden können.

  1. Expertenanleitung

Direkt von SAP-Experten lernen, die jahrelange Branchenerfahrung und tiefes Produktwissen mitbringen. Fragen werden in Echtzeit beantwortet, wodurch wertvolle Einblicke gewonnen werden können.

Inhalte des Kurses

  • Einführung in die SAP Build-Tools: Ein umfassender Überblick und sofortiger Praxisbezug.
  • Praktische Anwendungen: Erstellung funktionaler Anwendungen in kurzer Zeit.
  • Best Practices: Lernen der Dos and Don'ts der Low-Code-Entwicklung für effiziente und skalierbare Projekte.

Die Gelegenheit nicht verpassen!

Dies ist mehr als nur ein Kurs; es ist eine karriereverändernde Möglichkeit. Die Plätze sind begrenzt, also nicht warten – jetzt Platz für den 26. September und 12. November 2024 sichern!

👉Jetzt anmelden

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d869d7ab3caa48b2a20dc20dfa248380.html

if your app is v4, the standard change document component can only be integrated as popup screen.

entry.title: Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads
entry.content:
We are Konstantin Heine and Stephanie Stang, Product Managers for SAP BTP. We look forward to the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual – especially towards the SAP BTP General track.

Konstantin: As a child, I always played with interlocking building blocks. This led to my career, which now has to do with a modular platform. But jokes aside, I find it incredibly fascinating to see the multifaceted ways SAP BTP is utilized. In my role, I focus on making the development and operation of applications on SAP BTP easier, while also providing supporting assets to navigate certain challenges.

Stephanie:  While taking care of the SAP BTP cross roadmap topics and creating awareness for our customers, I discover every day what you can get out of SAP BTP. But even in private, I like to discover a lot. That’s why I like travelling around the world and getting to experience new adventures.

The SAP Business Technology Platform track is designed to provide an overview of the key technologies that make up the platform. We cover a broad range of topics and grouped them into three sub-tracks that help you to easily find sessions of interest:

  1. Strategy & Overview – Starting point to learn everything about SAP BTP and strategic topics.
  2. Guidance & Operations – Content on how to get started or advanced content on guidance and best practices.
  3. Security & Compliance – Contains sessions that help you understand how to securely operate and integrate your applications.

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

Our track provides insights into future trends with guidance on architecture, methodologies, and governance models to help define your path forward. Every SAP BTP application requires guidance for architecture, operations, and security. Regular discussions on these topics with customers and partners further enhance and improve our content.

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

In our SAP BTP General track, you’ll discover technology innovations that bring ideas to life. Sessions offer an overview of the platform’s key technologies, getting started guidance, best practices, and how to operate and securely integrate your applications. Begin with hands-on experience in the SAP Discovery Center or explore reference architectures within the SAP BTP Guidance Framework, covering your SAP BTP journey end-to-end.

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar)

  1. XP101 - SAP BTP strategy and highlights: See how SAP BTP brings together all pillars and get insights into our development priorities, explore upcoming innovation highlights, and come away with a clear picture of the platform’s future direction.
  2. XP105 - Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption: Learn how to get started with SAP BTP and what assets exist to design, develop, and operate enterprise-grade applications on SAP BTP.
  3. XP103 - Got security? Services for security and compliance with SAP BTP: Explore the services SAP BTP offers around security as well as identity and access management. 
  4. XP201 - Observability of custom-built CAP applications with SAP Cloud ALM: Learn how you can instrument your custom applications based on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), using OpenTelemetry.
  5. XP180 - Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center: This Jump-Start session allows you to explore the latest enhancements in speed of project implementation and personalization in SAP Discovery Center. 

And don’t miss the Track Overview session with both of us: XP100 - Unlock business innovation and achieve long-term success with SAP BTP.  Together, we will deep-dive into the sessions and discuss some of the highlights presented throughout the track.

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Coffee: Could be quite useful managing to watch the broad range of TechEd sessions.
  • Agenda Builder: Allows you to build your individual agenda and avoid colliding sessions (this will probably happen, but don’t worry as you can watch the recordings later).
  • Excitement and a lot of questions: Make sure to use the Q&A tool during sessions. We will be answering questions live in the session and in the tool.

Excited for what you will gain out of our track? Then join us at SAP TechEd Virtual in October and register today.

entry.title: Re: Summaries containing string
entry.content:
Given that InvalidNumberCheck is a formula, you should refer to it as {@InvalidNumberCheck} rather than  {InvalidNumberCheck}

Better yet, use the formula editor to navigate and insert it into the formula expression, instead of trying to type it yourself.

entry.title: Re: 3 Decimal places for Data Migration in S4 Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Dineshkumar,

for which objects do have this problem? Only for Open items AP or also for AR and G/L open items?

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Hello 

Issues where MRP generates a new purchase requisition are very often related to the rescheduling check. If your purchase order is outside the rescheduling horizon or not included in the rescheduling check, MRP might have to create a new purchase requisition to cover the reorder point. This WIKI talks about the rescheduling check:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697863.html

Another possibility is that your purchase order is not relevant to MRP. If the available quantity column is blank for your PO in transaction MD04, it means that it is not relevant to MRP, and that's why a new requisition can be created.

Regards,

Caetano

entry.title: Re: SAP Open Connectors With Cloud Integration Scenario
entry.content:
Hello,

can you explain why you haven't used the OpenConnectors adapter in the integration flow?

Best Regards
Gregor
